'''Louis Brogan''' (born 6 May 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]] and [[RFL League One]]. Brogan has also spent time on loan from [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Betfred Championship]].<ref name="itsrugby">{{cite web|url=https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/louis-brogan-47053.html|title=Louis BROGAN - Player statistics|website=It's rugby|access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

==Background==
Brogan was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester]], England. He is of Irish heritage, with family hailing from [[Donegal (town)|Donegal]].<ref name="donegallive">{{cite web|url=https://www.donegallive.ie/news/sport/490301/rugby-league-player-s-strong-donegal-town-links.html|title=Rugby League player's strong Donegal Town links|website=Donegal Live|date=31 October 2019 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref> He was educated at [[Leigh St Mary’s High School]].<ref name="leighleopards">{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/louis-brogan-is-a-leopard/|title=Louis Brogan is a Leopard|website=Leigh Leopards|access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

He played for [[Leigh Miners Rangers]] as a junior, graduating through their ranks.<ref name="leighminersrangers">{{cite web|url=http://www.leighminersrangers.club/player/louis-brogan/|title=Louis Brogan {{pipe}} Leigh Miners Rangers {{pipe}} Community Rugby League Club|website=leighminersrangers.club|access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

Brogan played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]] and was also ranked as their ‘Player of the Year’. He also played for the England Youth side, ranking as the European Championships ‘Best Forward’ and selected in the ‘Team of the Tournament’.<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/louis-brogan-ready-to-make-impact-on-professional-game|title=Louis Brogan ready to make impact on professional game|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 December 2019 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

He is a fully qualified electrician.<ref name="loverugbyleague2">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-new-boy-discusses-surreal-move-to-boyhood-club-leaving-a-full-time-job-behind-clubs-unbelievable-transformation|website=loverugbyleague.com|title=Leigh Leopards new boy discusses 'surreal' move to boyhood club, leaving a full-time job behind & club's 'unbelievable' transformation |date=30 January 2024 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

==Career==
Brogan trained through the 2019 pre-season with the [[Leigh Leopards]], featuring in a single friendly game against the [[London Broncos]] early in the year.<ref name="loverugbyleague3">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-sign-hidden-gem-championship-star-and-local-lad-for-2024-im-over-the-moon|website=loverugbyleague.com|title=Leigh Leopards sign 'hidden gem' Championship star and local lad for 2024; 'I'm over the moon'|date=14 October 2023 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

He returned to amateur side Leigh Miners Rangers, moving into the professional game ahead of the 2019 season with the [[Swinton Lions]], debuting in the [[2020 RFL Championship]] season.<ref name="zee-on">{{cite web|url=https://www.zee-on.com/away-player/13-louis-brogan/65210d040df6490014f886db|author=Zeeon Mockup|title=13 Louis Brogan|website=Zeeon|access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

Brogan left the [[Swinton Lions]] at the end of the end of the [[2023 RFL Championship]] season and joined [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67111573|title=Leigh Leopards sign St Helens' Lewis Baxter and Louis Brogan from Swinton - BBC Sport|website=bbc.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

He spent March and April 2024 on loan from the [[Leigh Leopards|Leopards]] back at the [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

In May 2024 Brogan made his debut for the Leigh Leopards in the [[Super League|top flight]] against the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref name="co.uk2">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx7dyw01q66o|title=Super League: Leigh Leopards 28-28 Castleford Tigers - BBC Sport|website=bbc.co.uk|date=4 May 2024 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

In June 2024 he signed a three-year contract with Leigh, keeping him there until the end of the 2027 season.<ref name="leighleopards2">{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/louis-brogan-signs-a-three-year-deal/|title=Louis Brogan signs a Three-Year-Deal|website=Leigh Leopards|access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>

Brogan spent June and July 2024 on loan from the Leopards at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="widnesvikings">{{cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/vikings-sign-louis-brogan-on-two-week-loan-from-leigh-leopards/|title=Vikings Sign Louis Brogan on Two-Week Loan from Leigh Leopards |website=Widnes Vikings|date=18 June 2024 |access-date=2026-01-16}}</ref>
